tasks.title: Inspect Elixir project structure
tasks.description: Check whether the repository already contains a Mix project and identify the appropriate application/module entry point for a CLI.
acceptance: Project structure is understood and documented for implementation.
If no Mix project exists, the need to create one is identified.

tasks.title: Create or configure CLI entry point
tasks.description: Add a Mix escript configuration and define a main module/function that can be executed from the command line.
acceptance: mix.exs includes an escript configuration with a valid main_module.
The main module exposes a main/1 function.
Running the built escript invokes the CLI entry point.

tasks.title: Implement rainbow Hello World output
tasks.description: Print the text "Hello World" using ANSI color escape codes so each character or segment appears in rainbow colors.
acceptance: CLI output includes the exact text "Hello World".
Output uses multiple ANSI colors in rainbow order.
ANSI formatting is reset after the message.

tasks.title: Add usage documentation
tasks.description: Document how to build and run the CLI locally.
acceptance: README or equivalent documentation includes build instructions.
Documentation includes an example command to run the CLI.
Documentation mentions that output requires a terminal with ANSI color support.

tasks.title: Verify CLI behavior
tasks.description: Run the CLI and confirm it prints the expected rainbow-colored greeting without errors.
acceptance: The CLI builds successfully with Mix.
Executing the CLI exits with status 0.
The terminal displays "Hello World" in rainbow ASCII colors.
